revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八幡朝見神社]]
{{draft|en}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = 八幡朝見神社.JPG |en}} '''Hachiman Asami Shrine''' (八幡朝見神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|qid=Q273880|lt=Beppu, Ōita|1=Beppu}}, Japan.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last=bebeppu|date=2020-03-22|title=Hachiman asami shrine|url=https://bebeppu.com/en/asami-shrine/|access-date=2023-10-19|website=ビーベップ|language=en-US|en}}</ref> It is also referred to as '''Asami Hachimangū''' (朝見八幡宮). The shrine holds the designation of a former Ken-sha under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] and is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}.
It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!|en}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー|url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/|access-date=2023-04-02|website=jinja-gosyuin.com|en}}</ref>
It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web|last=Motegi|first=Sadazumi|title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions)|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776|accessdate=23 March 2010|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto|language=Japanese|en}}</ref>
The Hachiman Asami Shrine is in Beppu city. It was made in 1196.<ref name=":0" />
== Overview == Visitors see two trees called Meoto-sugi at the shrine's steps. People also name them Mon-sugi.<ref name=":0" />
There is an old camphor tree at the shrine. Next to this tree is a stone. People stand on this stone and look at the tree.<ref name=":0" />
Near the Torii gate are two cedar trees called Meotosugi. Couples walk between these trees.<ref name=":0" />
The shrine has a cobblestone path. There are stones shaped like a gourd and a sake cup on this path.<ref name=":0" />
The shrine has a spring called Mantaro Shimizu. This spring has water. Visitors can take this water.<ref name=":0" />
The shrine offers fortune slips. Some slips are in English.<ref name=":0" />
== Cultural significance == Many people visit the shrine during the New Year. There are stone statues at the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
There are Inari shrines at the area. Some are near the main hall and a pond called Shinjiga-ike.<ref name=":0" /> == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates: * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|lt=Ōsasagi no Mikoto|ja|大鷦鷯尊|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Emperor Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q313119}} (仁徳天皇, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}), * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} (応神天皇, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|足仲彦命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|1=Emperor Chūai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q179971}} (仲哀天皇, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}), * {{ill|Empress Jingū|lt=Okina-gatarashi-hime no Mikoto|ja|気長足姫命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (神功皇后, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}).<ref name="booklet_enkaku">{{cite journal|title=History of Hachiman Asami Shrine|journal=Beppu Cultural Properties Series|issue=No.12|publisher=Beppu City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/pdf/gakusyuu/bunkazai/booklet/no12.pdf|en}}</ref>
The shrine grounds also house subsidiary shrines, including: * '''Tenjin Shrine''', enshrining {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, * '''Inari Shrine''', dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, * '''Itsukushima Shrine''', honoring {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, * '''Tenso Shrine''', venerating ancestral deities.<ref name="booklet_enkaku" />
== History == Hachiman Asami Shrine traces its origins to {{ill|1196|en|1196|qid=Q19747|lt=1196|1=1196}} during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. It was founded by {{ill|Ōtomo_Yoshinao|en|Ōtomo_Yoshinao|qid=Q11433255|lt=Ōtomo Yoshinao|1=Ōtomo Yoshinao}}, who brought deities from the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qid=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in Kamakura when he became governor of Bungo and Buzen provinces.<ref name="booklet_enkaku" /> A priest named Kō (formerly Fukuda), accompanying the transfer, became the shrine’s first priest, and his descendants continue to serve in this role.<ref name="about">{{Cite web|title=About Hachiman Asami Shrine|url=http://www.asami.or.jp/about.php|publisher=Hachiman Asami Shrine|accessdate=2020-02-20|en}}</ref>
The shrine was originally located on Ryūgaoka Hill (modern-day Kamihara Town, Beppu), but a volcanic eruption of Mount Tsurumi forced its relocation to its current site in {{ill|1348|en|1348|qid=Q6085|lt=1348|1=1348}}.<ref name="about" /><ref name="booklet_nenpyo">{{cite journal|title=Chronology of Hachiman Asami Shrine|journal=Beppu Cultural Properties Series|issue=No.12|url=https://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/pdf/gakusyuu/bunkazai/booklet/no12.pdf|en|qq=draft}}</ref>
The shrine flourished under the patronage of the Ōtomo clan but faced decline following their downfall in {{ill|1593|en|1593|qid=Q6772|lt=1593|1=1593}}. During the Edo period, the shrine was rebuilt and expanded multiple times. It gained prominence as a community center and was formally designated a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} under the modern Ranking system.<ref name="booklet_enkaku" />
After World War II, the {{ill|Onsen Shrines|lt=Onsen Shrines|lt=Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Shrine|qq=}}, which protected the Beppu hot springs, was merged with Hachiman Asami Shrine. The shrine continues to serve as a focal point for the annual {{ill|Beppu Hatto Onsen Festival|ja|別府八湯温泉まつり|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu Hatto Onsen Festival|qq=}} held in April.<ref name="about" />
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kusunoki (camphor) and Aragashi (quercus) forest of Hachiman Asami Shrine''': Designated a natural monument by Ōita Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Designated Cultural Properties of Beppu City|url=https://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/gakusyuu/bunkazai/bunkazai_shiteibunkazai.html|en}}</ref> *: The forest was also designated as a special protected forest in 1974.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/attachment/100227.pdf|title=Ōita Prefecture Environmental White Paper|publisher=Ōita Prefecture|date=2024|accessdate=2024-09-02|en}}</ref>
== Festivals == * '''Beppu Hatto Onsen Festival''': Early April. * '''Annual Grand Festival''': October 19.
== Access == * '''Address''': Asami 2-chōme, Beppu City, Ōita Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': 10 minutes by taxi from JR {{ill|Beppu Station|ja|別府駅|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=別府駅|qid=Q23775066}} or accessible via local bus routes.
== External Links == * [http://www.asami.or.jp Official Website] * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/pdf/gakusyuu/bunkazai/booklet/no12.pdf Booklet: Beppu Cultural Properties No.12]|en}}, Beppu City Board of Education. == References == {{reflist|en}}

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name--><!--Hachiman Asami Shrine--><!--this page is not from a simple english wikipedia draft. Categorizing it as such was an error. It is from an archived english wikipedia draft--><!--Hachiman Asami Shrine--><!--Hachiman Asami Shrine-->
